items.text: I prefer roles where I work with physical tools, equipment, or hardware rather than just screens.
I enjoy troubleshooting technical issues hands-on rather than delegating to support.
I'd take a role in field operations or site work over a purely desk-based position.
I like setting up infrastructure — servers, networks, lab environments — from scratch.
I find it satisfying to build or assemble something tangible at work.
I'd enjoy a job that requires me to be physically active rather than seated all day.
I prefer running deployments and configuring systems over writing documentation.
I enjoy DIY projects, home improvement, or working with my hands outside of work.
I regularly read industry research, whitepapers, or technical blogs beyond what my job requires.
I'd rather investigate the root cause of a production issue than apply a known workaround.
I enjoy designing experiments or proofs-of-concept to test a hypothesis at work.
I'm drawn to roles that require analyzing complex data to extract insights.
I prefer understanding the full architecture of a system over working on just my assigned component.
I'd enjoy a role in R&D, analytics, or strategy over execution-heavy operations.
I find it stimulating when my work involves evaluating multiple competing approaches.
I often question established processes and look for better ways to do things.
I enjoy creating presentations, pitch decks, or visual communications at work.
I often come up with creative solutions that aren't in the standard playbook.
I'd enjoy a role that involves content creation, branding, or design thinking.
I like customizing my workspace, tools, or workflows to reflect my personal style.
I prefer brainstorming sessions and ideation over following a fixed project plan.
I enjoy writing — reports, proposals, or even internal blogs — when I can bring my own voice to it.
I'm drawn to roles where innovation and original thinking are valued over following SOPs.
I've created something outside work — a blog, art, music, a side project — that I'm proud of.
I enjoy mentoring junior colleagues or conducting knowledge-sharing sessions.
I'm usually the person people approach for advice on career or personal matters at work.
I'd rather work in a people-facing role (training, HR, customer success) than a purely technical one.
I find it fulfilling when my work directly improves someone else's experience or outcome.
I enjoy facilitating team discussions and ensuring everyone's voice is heard.
I'd be interested in a career shift toward teaching, coaching, or counseling.
I prefer resolving interpersonal conflicts on my team over ignoring them.
I feel most engaged at work when I'm helping others grow or succeed.
I naturally take ownership of projects and drive them forward without being asked.
I enjoy pitching ideas to stakeholders, clients, or leadership.
I've seriously considered starting my own business or consultancy.
I'm energized by negotiations, deals, or competitive situations at work.
I'd enjoy a role where my compensation is tied to my ability to bring in business or close deals.
I prefer managing teams or projects over being an individual contributor.
I enjoy networking — conferences, LinkedIn, industry events — and building professional relationships.
I'm comfortable making high-stakes decisions with incomplete information.
I keep my tasks, emails, and files systematically organized.
I prefer clear SOPs and defined processes over ad-hoc ways of working.
I enjoy tasks that require precision — financial reporting, auditing, compliance checks.
I find it satisfying to create templates, checklists, or documentation that others can follow.
I'm comfortable in roles that require adherence to regulations, policies, or standards.
I'd rather have a predictable, structured workday than one that's constantly shifting.
I enjoy maintaining dashboards, tracking metrics, or managing inventories.
I take pride in delivering error-free, well-formatted work.

attention_checks.text: I have never used a computer or smartphone for any work task.
I am currently answering questions in an online assessment.
